---
title: "About"
description: "Background in banking analytics, quant-finance interests, and my AI research direction."
toc: true
---

> This page is an outline; I'm filling in the detail. The structure reflects what
> a prospective PhD supervisor scans for: background, focus, and direction.

## Professional background

[Short professional summary — your current role and what you do day to day.]

## Banking analytics

[Your analytics work in banking — the data, methods, scale, and the kinds of
problems you solve. This is concrete evidence of applied quantitative skill.]

## Quantitative finance interests

I work on systematic strategies, risk, and the statistics of financial markets.
My focus is on doing this *rigorously* — reproducible research, honest evaluation
(walk-forward, out-of-sample), and a clear-eyed view of where methods break down.

- [Interest 1 — e.g. selection bias and multiple testing in strategy discovery]
- [Interest 2 — e.g. volatility, regimes, and the cross-section of returns]
- [Interest 3 — e.g. machine learning for return prediction and the backtest-to-live gap]

## AI research direction

My developing research interest is in how **AI agents and large language models**
could support market decision-making under proper risk controls — not as black-box
signal generators, but as transparent, testable components of a trading system.
The [AI Trading Systems](ai-systems/index.qmd) and [PhD Research](phd/index.qmd)
sections develop this direction.

## Contact

- Email: [davidolivermaguire@gmail.com](mailto:davidolivermaguire@gmail.com)
- GitHub: [github.com/davidolivermaguire-ai](https://github.com/davidolivermaguire-ai)
- [LinkedIn / Google Scholar — optional]
